content:
- Task switch is complete and the remaining work is purely closeout: add/commit/push the rewritten contributor docs plus Ralph bookkeeping.
- I closed the testing worker cleanly; the two slower workers were interrupted because I had already integrated the relevant runtime/wiring and HA/API work locally.
- Commit message needs to capture that docs-lint/docs-build plus check/test/test-long/lint all passed, including the ultra-long HA scenarios and Docker smoke flows.

- Final state is complete and pushed on master as d00939e72f2f4f01e31075ab6f7d61682f6fb2e8.
- The contributor docs task is marked completed/passing, Ralph task switch ran successfully, and the worktree is clean after push.
- Gate evidence was fully green: docs-lint, docs-build, check, test, test-long, and lint; test-long also completed the Docker single-node and cluster smoke flows.

- Read progress log plus current active and done task summaries.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks; active candidates are two docs tasks, one low-priority hygiene task, and the recurring meta-task.
- Next I am checking the candidate task files for blocked_by tags, priorities, and any story-order signals so I can choose the correct next task and write it to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Checked candidate task files directly: no blocked_by tags are present on the active failing tasks.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/03-task-align-doc-file-order-and-names-with-rendered-site-structure.md as next because there are no active failing bugs, the project-wide hygiene task is explicitly low priority, and the README task says it belongs at the end of the docs usefulness story after the docs structure is cleaned up.
- Wrote the selected task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and am stopping immediately.

- Read the existing progress log and confirmed the previous task was fully closed out.
- Loaded .ralph/current_task.txt and found the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/03-task-align-doc-file-order-and-names-with-rendered-site-structure.md.
- Next I am reading that task file to detect whether it needs an initial plan, a skeptical verification pass, or direct execution.

- Explored docs/src, SUMMARY.md, and book.toml as required by the task before planning.
- Confirmed concrete navigation mismatches: Start Here points to docs/src/introduction.md instead of start-here/index.md, and Glossary is filed under concepts/ while rendered under Interfaces.
- Found extra empty docs/src directories (architecture, operations, testing, verification) that look like stale or abandoned naming experiments and should be considered in the plan.
- Next I am checking how other Ralph task files encode plan/review/execute markers so I can update the active task file in the expected house style.

- Read the active docs-tree task, confirmed it was in the initial planning phase, and completed the required exploration before planning.
- Verified concrete structural mismatches: introduction.md sits outside the Start Here chapter directory, glossary.md sits outside the Interfaces chapter directory, and several empty docs/src directories look stale.
- Patched the task file with a detailed Draft 1 execution plan, explicit future move targets, link-update/verification sequencing, skeptical review prompts, and the required terminal marker TO BE VERIFIED.
- I am stopping now because the user explicitly requires quitting immediately after the initial plan is written and marked TO BE VERIFIED.

- Read prior progress and confirmed the active task already has an initial plan pass recorded.
- Loaded .ralph/current_task.txt and am now checking whether the task file is in TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE state.
- Next step is to inspect the task markdown and follow the required phase exactly.

- Read the active docs-tree task in TO BE VERIFIED state and did a repo-backed skeptical review instead of accepting the draft plan as-is.
- Re-checked docs/src, SUMMARY.md, path references, and Makefile gates; confirmed the two planned moves are still correct and the stale docs/src directories are still empty.
- Changed the plan materially before promotion to NOW EXECUTE: added a required docs/src-vs-SUMMARY parity check, added make docs-hygiene to the verification stack alongside make docs-build, and explicitly documented that operator/ and assurance/ should not be renamed in this task.
- Next pass should read NOW EXECUTE and execute the plan directly without broad re-exploration.

- Read prior progress log and current task pointer.
- Active task is .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/03-task-align-doc-file-order-and-names-with-rendered-site-structure.md.
- Next I am reading the task file to detect whether this turn must plan, skeptically verify, or execute.

- Read the active task file in NOW EXECUTE state and validated the two planned docs mismatches are still live.
- Inspected the exact target files and live docs references before editing.
- Starting execution now: moving introduction into start-here/index.md and glossary into interfaces/glossary.md, then updating SUMMARY and sweeping remaining live refs.

- Executed the two planned docs moves and updated docs/src/SUMMARY.md to the new paths.
- Verified docs/src has no live references to introduction.md or concepts/glossary.md anymore.
- Ran the parity check between docs/src/SUMMARY.md and live markdown pages: no orphans, no missing nav targets. Removing stale empty docs/src directories next, then starting the verification gates.

- Docs execution work is complete: files moved, SUMMARY updated, stale empty dirs removed, task status set in_progress.
- docs/src parity check is green and both docs gates passed: make docs-build, make docs-hygiene.
- Starting the heavier verification stack now with make check, then make test, make test-long, and make lint.

- Verification stack is nearly complete and fully green so far.
- Passed: make docs-build, make docs-hygiene, make check, make test (428 passed, 9 skipped by profile), and make test-long (9 ultra-long passed, plus compose config and both docker smoke flows).
- Running make lint now. If it passes, the only remaining work is task file completion, Ralph task switch, commit, and push.
